chunks.text: The society of the twenty - first century carries an unprecedented complexity.
Climate change, pandemics, economic crises, political division.
There is no longer any problem that a single country can cope with on its own.
To face these extraordinary challenges, international cooperation is indispensable.
But in reality, each nation often gives priority to its own interest.
The extent of globalization has become deeper than ever.
A financial crisis in one country expands across the world in an instant.
An infectious disease in one region transfers across continents in weeks.
In such an interconnected world
it makes no sense to distinguish between " us " and " them.
" The suffering of someone will, in time, become the suffering of someone else.
For the sake of future generations, we need a definite commitment.
Reasonable action against climate change, initiative to end poverty, efforts toward peace.
What one individual can do looks small at first glance.
But when the choices of billions of individuals gather, the world surely changes.
Daily consumption, voting, posts on social media.
These are all vote that shape the future.
Hope is not optimism.
Hope is a commitment to action.
No matter how harsh the circumstances, to keep moving for a better future.
That is the small and great responsibility of each one of us.
